doc_title: California State Bar Emergency Access to Justice Protocol 2025 (EAJP-2025)
label: fabricated
reason: No such "Emergency Access to Justice Protocol 2025" exists from the California State Bar; the concept of a sub-regulatory protocol waiving Business & Professions Code §6125 UPL provisions, combined with numerous substantive implausibilities
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 93
REASON: No such "Emergency Access to Justice Protocol 2025" exists from the California State Bar; the document's structure, terminology (e.g., "unauthorized practice waiver," ABA-approved paralegal certificate requirement, post-conviction relief under §1170(d) bundled with civil matters), and the extraordinary claim of waiving UPL protections via an internal protocol all indicate this is an invented document rather than genuine institutional material.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 93
REASON: No such "Emergency Access to Justice Protocol 2025" exists from the California State Bar; the concept of a sub-regulatory protocol waiving Business & Professions Code §6125 UPL provisions, combined with numerous substantive implausibilities, strongly indicates this is an invented document.
